signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hike + COT vs OIS Divergence + G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: ECB hikes interest rates for the first time since 2023, driving EUR/USD to 1.1571. This move contradicts EUR COT LF net short −22,320 (specs fading a hawkish CB) vs ECB hike bias. If ECB maintains hawkish stance, a significant short-squeeze risk is elevated for EUR/USD. The G10 scorecard shows USD +0.607% vs AUD -0.943% (spread 1.550%), but the ECB hike provides a strong counter-narrative for EUR. Watch for further ECB commentary on 23 Jul.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Intervention Risk + Carry + COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.25 has broken above the 160.00 level, which has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk is now elevated. The 286bp Fed-BoJ carry premium (SOFR 3.59% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to sustain the bid, despite JPY COT LF net short −105,136 (specs fading a hawkish CB) vs BoJ hike bias. This extreme speculative short positioning against a hawkish BoJ creates significant short-squeeze risk for JPY (downside for USD/JPY) if the BoJ acts or signals a hike. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 50% hike priced) on 16 Jun is the immediate catalyst.

signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Volatility + CESI
signals.text: AUD is the weakest G10 currency this week, with AUD 1W avg -0.943% (n=4 pairs) vs USD 1W avg +0.607% (n=7 pairs), creating a 1.550% leader-to-laggard spread. This weakness is compounded by AUD CESI net -9 (data disappointing). AUD/USD IV at 27.0% vs HV30 at 8.1% shows an extreme +18.9% VRP, indicating strong hedging demand for AUD downside protection. The RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun is a key watch for any shift in stance that could alleviate pressure.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break + Trade Friction
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3976 sees extreme retail sentiment with 89% short, diverging from COT LF net short −49,052 (institutional short). This creates contrarian reversal risk if retail capitulates. The pair's correlation with WTI Oil has broken, with corr=-0.184 vs 252d norm=+0.013 (z=-1.72σ), indicating CAD is not benefiting from oil price movements as historically expected. Adding to pressure, the US-Canada delay of the Gordie Howe bridge opening signals renewed Trump-era trade friction. Watch for BoC commentary on 15 Jul.
evidence: US, Canada delay Gordie Howe bridge (headline)

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail Extreme + Accommodative Real Rate
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5818 shows extreme retail positioning with 88% long, indicating potential contrarian reversal risk if these positions unwind. The RBNZ's real policy rate is -0.83% (CB rate 2.25% vs inflation expectations 3.08%), signaling a mildly accommodative stance that offers little fundamental support for NZD strength. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 8 Jul is the key event for directional clarity.
